business_goal: Decide whether an external tool, MCP, official API, open-source library, low-code workflow, SaaS platform, or minimal glue code should implement this capability.

candidate_tools.label: Existing cross-border route runtime
candidate_tools.coverage: Reuse existing schemas, runtime outputs, control surfaces, and validation scripts when sufficient.
candidate_tools.data_freshness: depends on latest generated runtime artifact
risk_notes: May be draft-only and require additional evidence before real execution.

candidate_tools.label: Official API candidate
candidate_tools.coverage: Use when the source provides official structured access.
candidate_tools.license_or_cost: record free tier, key requirement, or paid plan
candidate_tools.data_freshness: record source update date or API response timestamp
risk_notes: Credentials and terms review may be required.

selected_path.reason: Use the highest priority option that satisfies the business goal with the least new code.

rejected_paths.reason: Rejected unless all higher-priority tool paths fail and the failure evidence is recorded.

evidence_requirements.freshness: Current-date verification required for compliance, tariffs, platform rules, pricing, and APIs.

implementation_ban: No real external send by default.
No customer outreach, quote send, ad spend, payment instruction, filing, or shipment booking without explicit human confirmation.
No bypassing login, captcha, paywall, platform policy, or robots restrictions.
No custom code before existing tools, MCP, official APIs, open-source libraries, low-code workflows, and SaaS options are checked.

fallback_plan.fallback_action: Record API/key/terms blocker and use broad public web search only to fill evidence gaps.
Return to product intake and request missing specifications, materials, certificates, price basis, images, or usage scenarios.

notes: Template only. Duplicate this file into a product or node-specific runtime folder before execution.
